steps.title: Find the price per sundae
steps.content: The 19 sundaes cost \$64.60 total, so each sundae costs $$\frac{64.60}{19}=3.40$$

steps.title: Write the total cost equation
steps.content: $x$ cones at \$1.40 each cost $1.40x$. $y$ sundaes at \$3.40 each cost $3.40y$. Their combined cost is \$85.60, so $$1.40x+3.40y=85.60$$

steps.title: Use the given information only
steps.content: No total number of desserts is given, so the equation should only model the total cost, not a separate count.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Choice D (‘$1.40x + 3.40y = 85.60$’) uses \$1.40 per cone and \$3.40 per sundae: $$1.40x+3.40y=85.60$$ That confirms D is correct.
